agent.name: build-deploy-auditor
agent.role: LYRA Agent F — build/deploy and observability: CI/CD, env, error boundaries, logging, deployment safety.

files_skipped: services/** Python repair service (deploy Dockerfile/railway not audited in this pass)
Full grep for empty catch blocks across all packages (spot-checked; no empty catch {} in apps/)
coverage.incomplete_reason: No .github workflows to review; focused on monorepo tooling, dashboard/worker entrypoints, Netlify/Vercel config, and preflight artifacts.

findings.title: Repository has no CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: There is no `.github/workflows/` directory and no automated jobs to run lint, typecheck, tests, or build on push/PR. Root `package.json` defines `lint`, `typecheck`, `test`, and `build` via Turbo, but nothing enforces them before merge or deploy.

proof_hooks.summary: Scripts exist but unrunnable in CI
proof_hooks.value: pnpm run lint | typecheck | test | build — no matching automation.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a GitHub Actions workflow (or other hosted CI) that installs pnpm, caches store, and runs turbo lint, typecheck, test, and build on pull_request and main.
steps: pnpm run lint && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run test && pnpm run build

findings.title: Dashboard ESLint config imports a package not declared in dependencies
findings.description: `eslint.config.mjs` imports `FlatCompat` from `@eslint/eslintrc`, but `apps/dashboard/package.json` does not list `@eslint/eslintrc`. `pnpm run lint` fails with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND, so the lint gate is broken for local dev and would fail in CI once added.

suggested_fix.approach: Add `@eslint/eslintrc` (and any peer deps ESLint 9 flat config needs) to `apps/dashboard` devDependencies, or replace FlatCompat with native flat config without @eslint/eslintrc.

findings.title: Next.js build warns that next.config traces the whole project (NFT)
findings.description: Production build output reports `Encountered unexpected file in NFT list` for `next.config.ts`, citing filesystem operations and an import chain through `lib/onboarding.ts` to an API route. That pattern risks oversized serverless traces or incorrect file inclusion for deploy targets that rely on NFT output.

proof_hooks.value: next.config.ts — Encountered unexpected file in NFT list — import trace includes app/api/.../onboarding/cluster/route.ts

suggested_fix.approach: Scope filesystem reads in config and shared modules to a dedicated data directory, gate `mergeEnvLocal` to development-only, or refactor onboarding cluster route to avoid pulling next.config into the server bundle trace.
steps: Use static paths or turbopackIgnore comments as suggested by the build output

findings.title: Deployment target ambiguity: Netlify config coexists with Vercel project file
findings.description: `netlify.toml` defines the dashboard build (`turbo run build --filter penny-dashboard`) and Next plugin, while `apps/dashboard/vercel.json` specifies framework, output directory, and npm-based install/build. It is unclear which platform is authoritative for production, which affects env var naming, build commands, and review of deployment safety.

suggested_fix.approach: Document the single source of truth for dashboard hosting; remove or archive the unused platform file to avoid drift.
steps: Decide Netlify vs Vercel for dashboard
Align lockfile/install commands with pnpm workspace

findings.title: Turbo lint does not cover worker TypeScript
findings.description: `apps/worker/package.json` has no `lint` script. Turbo runs `lint` only in workspaces that define it, so `pnpm run lint` exercises the dashboard only, not `apps/worker/src`.

proof_hooks.value: scripts: start, dev, build, admin — no lint

suggested_fix.approach: Add eslint (or biome) for `apps/worker` and a `lint` script; register in turbo.json inputs if needed.

findings.title: vercel.json install command does not match pnpm monorepo
findings.description: `apps/dashboard/vercel.json` sets `installCommand` to `npm install` while the repository uses `pnpm` and commits `pnpm-lock.yaml`. A Vercel project using this file may install inconsistent dependency trees and miss workspace hoisting.

suggested_fix.approach: Use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` from repo root or rely on Vercel’s pnpm detection; align with packageManager in root package.json.
steps: Document monorepo root for Vercel project settings

findings.title: next/font Google Fonts fetch can fail builds without network access
findings.description: The dashboard uses `next/font/google` in `app/layout.tsx`. The captured build failed when fonts.googleapis.com was unreachable (sandbox/offline). CI and reproducible builds should tolerate pinned fonts or self-hosted assets.

proof_hooks.value: Failed to fetch `Inter` from Google Fonts — next/font: error
pnpm run build — dashboard failed in restricted network environment

suggested_fix.approach: Self-host font files under `public/` or use `next/font/local`, or ensure CI has outbound HTTPS to fonts.googleapis.com.
steps: Evaluate local font files vs remote

next_actions.action: Add CI (e.g. GitHub Actions) running turbo lint, typecheck, test, and build with pnpm frozen lockfile.
Fix dashboard ESLint by adding @eslint/eslintrc (or refactor eslint.config.mjs) so `pnpm run lint` passes.
Resolve Next.js NFT / next.config tracing warning before relying on slim serverless bundles.
Decide Netlify vs Vercel for the dashboard and align secondary config files.
Add worker lint coverage and align vercel.json with pnpm if Vercel is used.
Harden font loading for offline/CI builds if reproducible builds are required.
